ideal_must_detect.matched_text: may constitute MNPI under UK MAR
any restricted transfers are made using the UK International Data Transfer Agreement or Addendum
The following examples are not linked to an identified person and should not be treated as personal data in isolation
we do not publish personal email addresses or device identifiers
Mr. Rowan Pike (born 14/02/1987), Associate Director (M&A) at Kestrelgate Investments plc
If you submit a data subject access request
In responding to requests we may redact or defer disclosure where necessary to protect MNPI, the rights of other individuals, or the detection and investigation of market abuse under UK MAR.

must_detect.matched_text: In responding to requests we may redact or defer disclosure where necessary to protect MNPI, the rights of other individuals, or the detection and investigation of market abuse under UK MAR.

Mr. Rowan Pike (born 14/02/1987), Associate Director (M&A) at Kestrelgate Investments plc, signed into the Bridgemont workspace (zone code LON-Z3, postcode XX1 1XX) on device KG-LON-14-AX3 using staff ID KG-031422 and OMS session OMS-2024-05-18-1142.
For Project Bramble (an unpublished potential equity placing), Mr. Pike’s access rights and chat handle rowan.pike@kestrelgate-plc.co.uk were recorded alongside account short code RC-4421 and call bridge reference CB-1179; these records may constitute MNPI under UK MAR.
The following examples are not linked to an identified person and should not be treated as personal data in isolation: a date 17/08/1985, a device tag OPS-TAB-02, or a generic job title “analyst” located in postcode YY9 9YY.
We engage processors such as Cloudhollow Analytics UK Ltd and Quarrygate Data Rooms Ltd under written terms; any restricted transfers are made using the UK International Data Transfer Agreement or Addendum and subject to risk assessment.
Insider lists and deal access logs are retained for five years after the end of the event per UK MAR; employee surveillance alerts and order-management logs are retained for seven years to meet financial-services recordkeeping.
We may collect subtle telemetry such as sign-in times (e.g., 07:43 and 18:10), hostname and OS version, and geofence markers (e.g., Wharf JN15), which are combined with training records to monitor market-abuse controls.
We do not make decisions producing legal or similarly significant effects solely by automated means; surveillance models flag behaviours for human review in our Compliance Operations Centre.
If you submit a data subject access request, contact privacy@kestrelgate-plc.co.uk or write via https://privacy.kestrelgate-plc.co.uk/dsar; we will verify identity using an employee number and a one-time code.
In responding to requests we may redact or defer disclosure where necessary to protect MNPI, the rights of other individuals, or the detection and investigation of market abuse under UK MAR.
We may share limited details with brokers, advisers, or venue operators on a need-to-know basis for transaction settlement and admission to trading, but we do not publish personal email addresses or device identifiers.
A generic account reference like 88-40-XX used in a training screenshot, or a session tag PRE-OPEN-07:55, is not used to identify any person and is stored only for illustrative purposes.
Queries may also be directed to the Data Protection Lead, Ms. Eliza North
must_detect.reason: promoted from strict runtime finding during Stage B baseline reconciliation

must_detect.matched_text:
We process employment and trading-related data to administer HR, operate surveillance, maintain insider lists, and manage deal rooms in connection with a UK regulated market; lawful bases are legal obligation and legitimate interests.
Example—Mr. Rowan Pike (born 14/02/1987), Associate Director (M&A) at Kestrelgate Investments plc, signed into the Bridgemont workspace (zone code LON-Z3, postcode XX1 1XX) on device KG-LON-14-AX3 using staff ID KG-031422 and OMS session OMS-2024-05-18-1142.
For Project Bramble (an unpublished potential equity placing), Mr. Pike’s access rights and chat handle rowan.pike@kestrelgate-plc.co.uk were recorded alongside account short code RC-4421 and call bridge reference CB-1179; these records may constitute MNPI under UK MAR.
The following examples are not linked to an identified person and should not be treated as personal data in isolation: a date 17/08/1985, a device tag OPS-TAB-02, or a generic job title “analyst” located in postcode YY9 9YY.
We engage processors such as Cloudhollow Analytics UK Ltd and Quarrygate Data Rooms Ltd under written terms; any restricted transfers are made using the UK International Data Transfer Agreement or Addendum and subject to risk assessment.
Insider lists and deal access logs are retained for five years after the end of the event per UK MAR; employee surveillance alerts and order-management logs are retained for seven years to meet financial-services recordkeeping.
We may collect subtle telemetry such as sign-in times (e.g., 07:43 and 18:10), hostname and OS version, and geofence markers (e.g., Wharf JN15), which are combined with training records to monitor market-abuse controls.
We do not make decisions producing legal or similarly significant effects solely by automated means; surveillance models flag behaviours for human review in our Compliance Operations Centre.
If you submit a data subject access request, contact privacy@kestrelgate-plc.co.uk or write via https://privacy.kestrelgate-plc.co.uk/dsar; we will verify identity using an employee number and a one-time code.
In responding to requests we may redact or defer disclosure where necessary to protect MNPI, the rights of other individuals, or the detection and investigation of market abuse under UK MAR.

uncertain.reason: Potential location/device zone code tied to a sign-in context but no explicit rule for UK zone codes
Micro-location tag can be linkable telemetry but not covered by existing rules
Postcode can be a quasi-identifier but there is no UK postal code rule in the allowed set
